---
title: "FinalProject2"
author: "Augie Jones, Mikey Pole and Luis Jiang"
date: "2026-05-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
games <- read.csv("data/games_march2025_cleaned.csv")

```

## Set up

```{r}
games <- games |>
  filter(metacritic_score > 0, price > 0, median_playtime_forever > 0, average_playtime_forever > 0)
games <- games |>
  mutate(success_score = num_reviews_total * price, log_success = log(success_score))

nrow(games)

median(games$metacritic_score)
median(games$pct_pos_total)
```

## Sample Description

```{r}
ggplot(data = games, aes(x = price)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black", binwidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$price, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$price, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = games, aes(x = metacritic_score)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black", binwidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$metacritic_score, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$metacritic_score, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = games, aes(x = pct_pos_total)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black", binwidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$pct_pos_total, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$pct_pos_total, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = games, aes(x = median_playtime_forever)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$median_playtime_forever, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$median_playtime_forever, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)

ggplot(data = games, aes(x = average_playtime_forever)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$average_playtime_forever, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$average_playtime_forever, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)

ggplot(data = games, aes(x = success_score)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.3, col = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(games$success_score, na.rm = T), col = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(games$success_score, na.rm = T), col = "red", size = 1.5)
```

## Question 1

```{r}
m_meta <- lm(log_success ~ metacritic_score, data = games)
glance(m_meta)
plot(m_meta)

m_user <- lm(log_success ~ pct_pos_total, data = games)
glance(m_user)
plot(m_user)

ggplot(data = games, aes(x = metacritic_score, y = log_success)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
ggplot(data = games, aes(x= pct_pos_total, y = log_success)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
```

## Question 2

```{r}
m_combined <- lm(log_success ~ metacritic_score + pct_pos_total, data = games)
glance(m_combined)
plot(m_combined)
```



## Question 3

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# grepl(pattern, x) returns TRUE/FALSE for each element of x —
# TRUE when the pattern appears in the string. Not covered in the notes,
# but it's the minimal tool needed to check "does this game's genres
# field mention Action?" etc.

# Step 1: add one TRUE/FALSE column per genre using mutate()
games <- games |>
  mutate(
    is_action     = grepl("Action",     genres),
    is_adventure  = grepl("Adventure",  genres),
    is_casual     = grepl("Casual",     genres),
    is_indie      = grepl("Indie",      genres),
    is_rpg        = grepl("RPG",        genres),
    is_simulation = grepl("Simulation", genres),
    is_strategy   = grepl("Strategy",   genres),
    is_sports     = grepl("Sports",     genres),
    is_racing     = grepl("Racing",     genres)
  )

# Step 2 & 3 combined: for each genre, filter to games in that genre,
# then summarize. Build one row per genre with data.frame() and c().
# This uses only filter(), mean(), median(), and the pipe — all in the notes.

action_games     <- games |> filter(is_action)
adventure_games  <- games |> filter(is_adventure)
casual_games     <- games |> filter(is_casual)
indie_games      <- games |> filter(is_indie)
rpg_games        <- games |> filter(is_rpg)
simulation_games <- games |> filter(is_simulation)
strategy_games   <- games |> filter(is_strategy)
sports_games     <- games |> filter(is_sports)
racing_games     <- games |> filter(is_racing)

genre_summary <- data.frame(
  genre = c("Action", "Adventure", "Casual", "Indie", "RPG",
            "Simulation", "Strategy", "Sports", "Racing"),
  n = c(
    length(action_games$success_score),
    length(adventure_games$success_score),
    length(casual_games$success_score),
    length(indie_games$success_score),
    length(rpg_games$success_score),
    length(simulation_games$success_score),
    length(strategy_games$success_score),
    length(sports_games$success_score),
    length(racing_games$success_score)
  ),
  mean_success = c(
    mean(action_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(adventure_games$success_score,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(casual_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(indie_games$success_score,      na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(rpg_games$success_score,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(simulation_games$success_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(strategy_games$success_score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(sports_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(racing_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  median_success = c(
    median(action_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    median(adventure_games$success_score,  na.rm = TRUE),
    median(casual_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    median(indie_games$success_score,      na.rm = TRUE),
    median(rpg_games$success_score,        na.rm = TRUE),
    median(simulation_games$success_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    median(strategy_games$success_score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    median(sports_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    median(racing_games$success_score,     na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

genre_summary

# Step 4: plot. The notes only show geom_point, geom_histogram,
# geom_boxplot, geom_bar, geom_density. For a bar chart from a
# summary table, geom_col is the natural fit, but it's not in the notes.
# A clean alternative: just use geom_point — same information, and
# geom_point IS in the notes (section 2.4.1).
ggplot(data = genre_summary, aes(x = genre, y = mean_success)) +
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
m_genre <- lm(log_success ~ is_action + is_adventure + is_casual +
                            is_indie + is_rpg + is_simulation +
                            is_strategy + is_sports + is_racing,
              data = games)
plot(m_genre)
```

```{r}
tidy(m_genre)
glance(m_genre)
```

## Question 4

```{r}
m_price <- lm(log_success ~ price, data = games)
plot(m_price)
```

```{r}
cor(games$price, games$log_success, use = "complete.obs")
tidy(lm(log_success ~ price, data = games))
glance(lm(log_success ~ price, data = games))
ggplot(games, aes(price, log_success)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Q4: Price vs. log(success score)",
       x = "Price (USD)", y = "Success score (log scale)")
```

Since price appears in success_score by construction, I also checked the relationship using just review count as the outcome. 

```{r}
games <- games |>
  mutate(log_num_reviews = log(num_reviews_total))
tidy(lm(log_num_reviews ~ price, data = games))
glance(lm(log_num_reviews ~ price, data = games))

cor(games$price, games$log_num_reviews, use = "complete.obs")
ggplot(games, aes(price, log_num_reviews)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Q4: Price vs. log(number of reviews)",
       x = "Price (USD)", y = "Number of reviews (log scale)")
```


## Question 5

```{r}
games <- games |>
  mutate(log_median_playtime = log(median_playtime_forever))

games <- games |>
  mutate(log_average_playtime = log(average_playtime_forever))

m4 <- lm(log_success ~ log_median_playtime, data = games)
g4 <- glance(m4)
g4$adj.r.squared

plot(m4)
m5 <- lm(log_success ~ log_average_playtime, data = games)
g5 <- glance(m5)
g5$adj.r.squared

plot(m5)

ggplot(data = games, aes(x= log_median_playtime, y = log_success)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
ggplot(data = games, aes(x= log_average_playtime, y = log_success)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
```

## Question 6

```{r}
games <- games |>
  mutate(
    is_multiplayer = grepl("Multi-player", categories) & !is.na(categories),
    player_mode = if_else(is_multiplayer, "Multiplayer", "Single-player")
  )

games |>
  count(player_mode)

ggplot(data = games, aes(x = player_mode, y = log_success, fill = player_mode)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  xlab("Player mode") +
  ylab("Log of success score")

plot(lm(log_success ~ player_mode, data = games))

# Welch's t-test on log scale
t_res <- t.test(log_success ~ player_mode, data = games)
t_res
```

## Question 7 

```{r}
games <- games |>
  mutate(price_efficiency = median_playtime_forever/price)

games <- games |>
  mutate(log_price_efficiency = log(price_efficiency))


cor(games$success_score, games$price_efficiency, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
# 0.07304141
# Because it is close to 0, it suggests that there is almost no monotonic relationship between the success_score and price_efficiency

ggplot(data = games, aes(x = log_price_efficiency, y = log_success)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
```
